notes_md:
**hết hồn** = scared stiff, suddenly shocked so badly it feels like your “soul left your body.” It’s strong and dramatic.

It’s usually used as a verb phrase after a subject, or as a reaction on its own. Often followed by a reason.


Stronger than *giật mình* (just startled), similar to *hoảng hồn* but more emotional and everyday.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Last night I heard a strange noise and was scared stiff.
He saw a snake under the tree and was scared stiff.
She was scared stiff when her friends suddenly played a prank on her.
notes:
hết hồn = scared stiff, suddenly shocked so badly it feels like your “soul left your body.” It’s strong and dramatic.

It’s usually used as a verb phrase after a subject, or as a reaction on its own. Often followed by a reason.

Stronger than giật mình (just startled), similar to hoảng hồn but more emotional and everyday.
exampleSentences: Tối qua em nghe tiếng động lạ nên hết hồn luôn.
Anh nhìn thấy con rắn dưới gốc cây mà hết hồn.
Chị bị bạn bè trêu bất ngờ nên hết hồn một lúc.

definitions.vi: Cảm giác bị giật mình hoặc hoảng hốt bất ngờ.

example.vi: Anh ấy làm tôi hết hồn.
example.es: Me pegó un susto de muerte.
